evidence_excerpt: Chronic relapsing severe aplastic anaemia with platelet declines after cyclosporine taper (to 25 ×10^9/l in 1996 and to 49 ×10^9/l in 2001) requiring re‑initiation of cyclosporine; developed Graves’ disease treated with radioiodine.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Long-term course included hyperthyroid symptoms (palpitations, excessive sweating, poor weight gain) with exam showing tachycardia and exophthalmos; intermittent cytopenia relapses.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Transfusion independent by 6 weeks (Hb 8.2 g/dl, platelets 86 ×10^9/l, ANC 1.94 ×10^9/l).
After CSA taper in June 1996, platelet count fell to 25 ×10^9/l.
CSA resumed in September 1996 at 12 mg/kg/day orally.
Responded with gradually increasing platelet counts (152 ×10^9/l in March 1998).
CSA discontinued in May 1999; CBC remained normal thereafter for a period.
In April 2001 platelet count fell to 93 ×10^9/l; exam showed goitre, exophthalmos, tachycardia; Graves’ disease confirmed.
Treated with atenolol and 9.11 mCi of radioactive iodine (I-131).
CSA re‑instituted in September 2001 due to worsening platelet counts (49 ×10^9/l).

notes_on_uncertainty: Follow-up after radioiodine ablation and CSA re‑initiation (post‑September 2001) is not provided; sustained post-treatment symptom burden and functional status are not explicitly documented.
